story_summary: A reflective first-person account of a lifelong passion for vocabulary: influences, habits (collections, subscriptions, games), and the pleasure derived from words and their clarity.

context.role: early-career professional who later remained engaged with vocabulary (mentions first managing partner at Peat Marwick)
context.time_period: spans decades from early career to the present (references to 'years ago' and recent website activity)
context.stakes: personal intellectual fulfillment and lifelong learning rather than high organizational stakes

core_conflict: No high-stakes conflict; main tension is how to cultivate and sustain a personal avocation for words alongside other activities.

decision_moments.trigger: Years ago I started a collection of words that I happened to like.
decision_moments.choice: Create an official list on the computer and apply personal criteria for inclusion.
decision_moments.reasoning: It was just a labor of love that I found enjoyable.
decision_moments.risk_accepted: Time and attention invested in curating the list.
decision_moments.outcome: Built an official list of words that formed the basis for later sharing and learning.

decision_moments.trigger: A friend tipped me off to a website called Thinkmap Visual Thesaurus.
decision_moments.reasoning: It has been fascinating to begin each day with this short lesson.
decision_moments.risk_accepted: Subscription/time commitment to receive daily content.
decision_moments.outcome: Receives a 'Word of the Day' each day and learns new words (example: 'garbanzo').

decision_moments.trigger: Vocabulary.com published a 'Featured Word Lists' where readers submitted lists.
options: Submit a portion of the collection publicly
decision_moments.choice: Submitted a portion labeled '93 Eclectic Words'.
decision_moments.reasoning: Wanted to share; enjoyed watching the durability and reading comments.
decision_moments.risk_accepted: Public exposure and potential critique of the list.
decision_moments.outcome: List ranked number one for a while and remains ranked number nineteen on the site's Top Fifty; received dozens of reader comments.

decision_moments.trigger: Interest in improving vocabulary through engaging activities and social play.
decision_moments.choice: Plays competitive games daily (Words with Friends, Upwords) with friends and family.
decision_moments.reasoning: The competition is fun, but it is also an excellent learning experience.
decision_moments.risk_accepted: Time spent on games (described elsewhere as a 'silly waste of time' yet continued).
decision_moments.outcome: New words continue to pop up regularly; sustained practice and enjoyment; cumulative scores tracked (close results with opponent).

principles.principle: Regular, small exposures build vocabulary over time.
principles.evidence: I subscribed and through it receive a "Word of the Day" each day of the week

principles.principle: Curate a personal, persistent list to deepen familiarity with chosen words.
principles.evidence: Years ago I started a collection of words that I happened to like

principles.principle: Learning driven by intrinsic enjoyment sustains long-term practice.
principles.evidence: It was just a labor of love that I found enjoyable.

principles.principle: Use playful competition to reinforce learning and maintain engagement.
principles.evidence: I play this competitive game every day

principles.principle: Sharing work publicly can create durability and useful feedback.
principles.evidence: I submitted a portion of my word collection and labeled it "93 Eclectic Words".

decision_heuristics.heuristic: Capture interesting words immediately in a personal list on your computer.
decision_heuristics.evidence: Years ago I started a collection of words that I happened to like

decision_heuristics.heuristic: Subscribe to a daily 'Word of the Day' service to ensure regular exposure.
decision_heuristics.evidence: I subscribed and through it receive a "Word of the Day" each day of the week

decision_heuristics.heuristic: Use competitive word games daily with peers to learn new vocabulary through play.
decision_heuristics.evidence: I play this competitive game every day

decision_heuristics.heuristic: Publish a curated selection to public forums to test durability and gather feedback.
decision_heuristics.evidence: I submitted a portion of my word collection and labeled it "93 Eclectic Words".

leadership_patterns.risk_tolerance: Low-to-moderate; willing to accept small personal/time risks for intellectual enjoyment and learning.
leadership_patterns.communication_style: Precise, descriptive, and appreciative of nuanced language aimed at clarity.
leadership_patterns.accountability_posture: Self-directed ownership of learning and curation activities (initiates and maintains personal practices).
leadership_patterns.ethical_framing: Values clarity and correctness in language; frames vocabulary as a tool for clarity and power.

quotes.quote: It was just a labor of love that I found enjoyable.
They have life. They have power. They deliver clarity.
I subscribed and through it receive a "Word of the Day" each day of the week
Years ago I started a collection of words that I happened to like.
I play this competitive game every day with Dot
